content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 11, 1994

Robert E. Leidigh.
Olson, Hagel, Fong, Leidigh,
Waters and Fishburn
CapitolBank Center
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for
Confirmation of Telephone
Advice
Our File No. I 93-480

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.¹ Since your request is one for general
information, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).²

Your letter dated December 28, 1993, correctly summarizes my
advice that "occupation/employer" information is not required to
be disclosed for non-individual contributors (i.e., business
entities as well as political action committees) since that
information would not be applicable. The Commission indicated in
the Silverman Advice Letter, No. I-88-031, that "...because
business entities and recipient committees which make
contributions to candidates and committees do not have an
'occupation' as that term is generally used, nor an employer, the
Commission has advised candidates and committees that occupation
and employer information is not required for contributions
received from persons other than individuals."

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear in 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Leidigh, Robert E.
No. I 93-480
Page 2


If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Wayne Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: Whether occupation/employer information must be disclosed for contributions received from non-individual contributors, such as business entities and political action committees.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Occupation/employer information is not required to be disclosed for non-individual contributors (business entities and political action committees) since that information would not be applicable to entities that do not have an occupation or employer in the traditional sense.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' sections with headers, but the substantive question and answer are clearly stated in the letter body. Treated as informal assistance per Regulation 18329(c). Date discrepancy: document ID year 1993 but letter dated January 11, 1994.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether occupation/employer information must be disclosed for contributions received from non-individual contributors, such as business entities and political action committees.

CONCLUSION: Occupation/employer information is not required to be disclosed for non-individual contributors (business entities and political action committees) since that information would not be applicable to entities that do not have an occupation or employer in the traditional sense.
